title: Virtual reality or augmented reality headset
abstract: The present application relates to a virtual reality or augmented reality headset (10). The virtual reality or augmented reality headset (10) comprising: a display (101) for displaying an image to a user (20), the display (101) configured to be positioned at the front of the head of a user (20); a head mounting attachment (105), attached to the display (101), configured to support the display (101) through contact with the top of the head of the user (20) and the forehead of the user; wherein the head mounting attachment (105) comprises a movable joint (117), such that in use, the display (101) is pivotable relative to the head mounting attachment (105) about an axis that intersects the centre of both of the eyes of the user (20).
claims: Claims 1. A virtual reality or augmented reality headset comprising: a display for displaying an image to a user, the display configured to be positioned at the front of the head of a user; a head mounting attachment, attached to the display, configured to support the display through contact with the top of the head of the user and the forehead of the user; wherein the head mounting attachment comprises a movable joint, such that in use, the display is pivotable relative to the head mounting attachment about an axis that intersects the centre of both of the eyes of the user. 2. The headset of claim 1 , wherein the movable joint comprises a hinge. 3. The headset of any preceding claim, wherein the movable joint is a friction hinge. 4. The headset of any preceding claim, wherein the head mounting attachment comprises: a counterweight, configured to be positioned at the rear of the head of the user; and a connector connecting the display to the counterweight, such that in use, the connector is adapted to rest on the top of the head of the user to support the weight of the display and the counterweight, and so that the counterweight acts to counter the weight of the display. 5. The headset of claim 4, wherein the connector is elongate. 6. The headset of any of claim 4 or 5, comprising a plurality of connectors. 7. The headset of any of claims 4 to 6, wherein the connector is arcuate. 8. The headset of any of claims 4 to 7, wherein the counterweight comprises a control unit for generating the image, and providing the image to the display. 9. The headset of claim 8, wherein the connector comprises at least one recess extending between the counterweight and the display, and at least one cable within the at least one recess, the at least one cable for transmitting signals between the electronics and the display. 10. The headset of any of claims 4 to 9, wherein the connector is movable from a first shape to a second shape, wherein the second shape is biased towards the first shape, so that the connector can exert a gripping force on the head of the user in the second shape. 11. The headset of any of claims 4 to 10, wherein the connector comprises cushioning. 12. The headset of any claims 4 to 1 1 , further comprising a plurality of balancing members configured to contact the head of the user, such that when in use, the balancing members control the orientation of the display relative to an axis intersecting the centre of both of the eyes of the user. 13. The headset of claim 12, wherein the plurality of balancing members comprise a first balancing member and a second balancing member, the first and second balancing members configured in use to contact a respective ear of the user. 14. The headset of any of claims 4 to 13, wherein the counterweight comprises at least one pressure pad, such that when in use, the at least one pressure pad cradles the back of the head and extends along at least a portion of the sides of the head of the user. 15. The headset of claim 14, wherein the at least one pressure pad is padded. 16. A virtual reality or augmented reality headset comprising: a display for displaying an image to a user, the display configured to be positioned at the front of the head of the user; a counterweight, configured to be positioned at the rear of the head of the user; and a connector connecting the display to the counterweight, such that in use, the connector is adapted to rest on the top of the head of the user to support the weight of the display and the counterweight, and so that the counterweight acts to counter the weight of the display. 17. The headset of claim 16, wherein the counterweight comprises a control unit for generating the image, and providing the image to the display. 18. The headset of claim 17, wherein the connector comprises at least one recess extending between the counterweight and the display, and at least one cable within the at least one recess, the at least one cable for transmitting signals between the control unit and the display. 19. The headset of any of claims 16 to 18, wherein the connector is flexible, such that in use, it is configured to contact a plurality of regions of the head of the user, such that the support is distributed across the head of the user. 20. The headset of any of claims 16 to 19, wherein the connector is movable from a first shape to a second shape, wherein the second shape is biased towards the first shape, so that the connector can exert a gripping force on the head of the user in the second shape. 21. The headset of any of claims 16 to 20, wherein the connector is arcuate. 22. The headset of any of claims 16 to 21 , wherein the connector comprises cushioning. 23. The headset of any of claims 16 to 22, comprising a plurality of connectors, or a single connector. 24. The headset of any of claims 16 to 23, wherein the connector is elongate. 25. The headset of any of claims 16 to 24, further comprising a plurality of balancing members configured to contact the head of the user, such that when in use, the balancing members control the orientation of the display relative to an axis intersecting the centre of both of the eyes of the user. 26. The headset of claim 25, wherein the plurality of balancing members comprise a first balancing member and a second balancing member, the first and second balancing members configured in use to contact a respective ear of the user. 27. The headset of any of claims 16 to 26, wherein the counterweight comprises at least one pressure pad, such that when in use, the at least one pressure pad cradles the back of the head and extends along at least a portion of the sides of the head of the user. 28. The headset of claim 27, wherein the at least one pressure pad is padded.
